place.name: JUNO
place.full_address: 6 Kloof Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa

reviews.text: Ordered eggs Benedict, food looked good but had zero flavor. Hit and miss with this restaurant sometimes you go there and the food tastes like it’s been prepared by a chef other times the food tastes like it comes from an average catering company. :(
reviews.author: Lyndon Barry

reviews.text:
We booked accommodation at the Tropicana Hotel. The hotel charged us for 4 people instead of 5 because our youngest child is 4 years old. When we checked in, we were told we would have R780 in credit to spend on food, and that if we exceeded that amount, we would simply pay the difference. That sounded straightforward.
However, on the second day, we didn’t finish the food credit—and suddenly we were told that the remaining balance could not be used for anything else. No one had mentioned that before. Today, the situation became even more confusing: we were charged extra for drinks and other items, despite the fact that we still had unused credit from the R780. It feels as if the rules change depending on who is on duty at the time.
At this point, I honestly don’t know whether this is the standard experience for all guests or whether we were just an unfortunate Black family receiving inconsistent treatment.
To make matters worse, our return flight is early in the morning, so we won’t be able to have breakfast. Trying to get a refund or convert the breakfast voucher into a dinner voucher was another exhausting process, with staff sending us from one place to another with no clear answer. I don’t even know whether to blame the Tropicana Hotel or Juno, the restaurant, because the communication between them seems completely disorganized.
The constant back-and-forth, the inconsistency, and the feeling of being dismissed over something as basic as food and money has been extremely frustrating and honestly ruined a part of our stay.

I wouldn’t want to come back. The experience removed the convenience we hoped for especially traveling with kids.
reviews.author: Babalwa Nyamela

reviews.text:
We waited a long time just to have our orders taken. When the coffee finally came, it was cold and the milk was burnt. Not something we would normally complain about, but after such a long wait it was disappointing.

We asked to sit inward where most of the guests were seated but were told it was full. A few minutes later, certain people arrived and tables were moved around to accommodate them. We were placed by the salad bar. Next to us was another table also being ignored.

It was clear that people who knew the staff were getting preferred seating and better service. The lady working at the salad bar was the one calling on staff to help she could see I was annoyed an irritated by the lack of basic service.

Staff were arguing among themselves and the whole place felt disorganised.

The bacon in the eggs Benedict was burnt. For a dish that’s just an English muffin, bacon and an egg, it really shouldn’t be that difficult to get right.

If this is a restaurant that caters to regulars or cliques or elites or whatever was going on, then we need to be told membership is required. I’m happy to enjoy my Sunday brunch elsewhere.
reviews.author: Tebo Mabeba

reviews.text:
Shocking,Shocking…
If people want to come in groups without reservation, then they should come early to secure a table or wait until they can be accommodated.This is standard practice in the hospitality industry…but to be requested to move by restaurant staff manager, to accommodate  other clients whilst not having completed my breakfast (even if I have already paid my bill), is preposterous!!
What is even more unbelievable is that the order  for the group that had to be accommodated was prepared and brought to the table next to where I was sitting…without knowing how long I was going to be there…….
I think you should put up a notice to inform clients that they have a limited time in which to complete their meals or otherwise they will be asked to leave even if they did not complete their meals!! All this, as it appears, so you can turnaround tables as quickly as possible to make maximum profit…
We have never experienced this anywhere, or anytime even it was only drinking a coffee in a coffee shop, no matter how busy it was…
This was a most shocking and unpleasant experience for a start to a relaxing Sunday morning…
reviews.author: Elisabeth Parker

reviews.text: Wow, noch nie sowas erlebt in 25 Jahren, man hat nur 1,5h WLAN, danach ist es abgelaufen :) keine Chance mehr auf Verlängerung, auch nicht wenn man nochmal was bestellt oder so, haben nett gefragt. Mitten am arbeiten gewesen und es wurde abrupt beendet. Geht gar nicht, das stellt alles positive an diesem Ort in den Schatten und ich werde niemals mehr herkommen. Ganz abgesehen davon das der Laden komplett leer war, wie man a den Bildern erkennen kann. Selbst wenn man noch was bestellen muss, aber nein, selbst dann, kein an mehr 😂 absurd…, Wie man auf sowas kommt, ist mir ein Rätsel. Aber gut, einmal und nie wieder!
reviews.author: Dennis Di Pasquale

reviews.text:
Sehr nettes Lokal....Frisches essen.
Die Toats, Croissant und der Kaffee köstlich. Der Orangensaft frisch gepresst. Toller und sehr aufmerksamer Service.
Wir kommen auf jeden Fall wieder! Sehr empfehlenswert.
reviews.author: Sarah Nopper

reviews.text:
Overall, our experience fell short of expectations. The food itself was decent, though portions were quite small. Unfortunately, the service let the visit down.

On arrival, we waited to be seated and then waited a further 10 minutes before having to ask for menus. When ordering, only four out of six guests were initially taken, and the waitress began to walk away twice before the remaining two orders were prompted.

Service throughout the meal was slow and inconsistent. Drinks took around 15 minutes to arrive, food arriving first, some items were forgotten, meals had to be chased, and we had to remind multiple times about paying. Once requested, the card machine also took an additional 10 minutes to arrive.

There seemed to be a lack of organisation regarding who was responsible for our table, which made the experience feel disjointed. At one point, we were served drinks by a member of staff while she was on a phone call, which came across as unprofessional.
reviews.owner_answer:
Hi Matthew. 
Please can you share with us your email or contact number. We would love to get in touch with you.
Thank you so much.
Katie
reviews.author: Matthew McVeigh

reviews.text:
Sat for 20 minutes till we could finally get someone’s attention to order. Then advised that breakfast menu ended five minutes ago. Annoyed. So many staff and so few tables, really no reason.
Another 15 minutes to get the bill.
No sugar.
Also not sure why all of the staff are walking around with cleaning rags; a bit worried they are both cleaning and handling food.
reviews.author: Deven Blackburn

reviews.text: Overpriced and small food portions! They don't allow you to to use there wifi longer than 60 min. There are so many good breakfast/lunch restaurants in Sea Point where you pay half of what you do here (and get more food, and free access to wifi).
reviews.author: Oscar Cederin

reviews.text:
The food 🌝

Eggs on toast
“Cream cheese” and “salmon trout” 👀 bagel

The pictures can tell the food storyyy, on the other hand - vibes and aesthetic are immaculate 💛

(We ended uP returning the bagel as it was not edible, we were not charged)
reviews.author: Zoë

reviews.text: Juno’s space is beautiful, the menu has a nice variety of options, especially for vegetarians. The veg sandwich on focaccia was delicious and the chicken mayo on sourdough was really tasty as well. My only feedback would be the service. We waited 15 minutes for a waiter to notice us and bring a menu - manager was walking around but didn’t seem to have noticed us looking around waiting for service. After we ordered our coffees, she comes to ask if everything is okay, which seemed badly timed. Took 20 minutes to get a takeaway box for the sandwich. The food came out quickly but the place seems understaffed with the staff not being very attentive at all. They seem confused and overwhelmed.
reviews.author: Natasha Jacobs

reviews.text: Food was very good but portions were tiny for the price. I ordered the croissant French toast and received one piece of toast for R120. Similar for rest of our group. R125 for smashed avo on one very small piece of sourdough. Coffee also served in very small cups. Come on guys people want to start of the day with a decent breakfast, add an extra piece of toast. 😂
reviews.author: Jonathan Ruzow

reviews.text: Food was good, price was ok but we had to wait for a table. The place is good for breakfast and you will enjoy their focaccia bread.
reviews.author: Hannes van Rooyen

reviews.text:
This place is such a sweet little gem with genuinely excellent service. From the moment we stepped inside, we were greeted with warmth and kindness. Everyone made us feel right at home.

We weren’t in the mood for a full meal, so we kept it light, although the menu had plenty of tempting choices. We ended up trying their croissants, which were perfectly flaky and buttery. The coffee was smooth and satisfying, and I ordered a chai latte with oat milk. It was just the way I like it — frothy, balanced, and not too sweet. It paired beautifully with my mini croissant and made for a lovely little moment of calm.

The prices are very reasonable, and the overall vibe is peaceful and grounded. The space has a soft, earthy feel, and the noise level is just right for conversation or quiet reflection. It’s the kind of place you want to linger in, whether you’re catching up with a friend or enjoying a solo break.
reviews.author: Lewis Cardenas

reviews.text: We like it for brunch. Love their granola. Service was excellent.

sub_ratings.Seating type: Indoor dining area, Outdoor patio / terrace

reviews.author: Mel Simon

reviews.text:
Aesthetically pleasing - a lot of effort went into fitting the café and deli.
Had an americano, an almond croissant and freshly squeezed orange juice. Good roast, and the almond croissant, although not super flakey, wasn't lacking any filling or toppings (generous).

Staff were friendly and on top of things.

Recommend checking out the deli too!
reviews.author: Annemi du Toit

reviews.text:
I popped in the other day and the staff were so welcoming; the interior looks smart and is spacious with a lovely outdoor area.
Welcome to our neighbourhood.

I was disturbed to read a review mentioning that Mayphil Court 'residents' will be complaining and arguing about noise created by your establishment!

I would like this person NOT to assume that she has the right to speak on my behalf or anyone else's.

The fact is:  Mayphil Court has only one toxic resident/owner who is the habitual complainer.
reviews.author: Shireen Is

reviews.text: Fantastic service and the food is well prepared.
reviews.author: Ilze Wagenaar

reviews.text: Found ourselves eating here almost every morning during our stay in Seapoint, South Africa. The food, the service, the location, the atmosphere and the prices were excellent! The croissants tasted like they were freshly baked (daily), the lamb bacon was tender and even the eggs were richer than any egg I've ever eaten in then the world. Thank you for such a wonderful experience
reviews.author: Melba Johnson-Hargrove
